skill.name: datadog-automation
skill.description: Automate Datadog monitoring tasks via Rube MCP including metrics queries, log searches, monitor management, dashboard operations, events, and downtimes.
skill.summary: Streamline Datadog observability operations through Composio's Datadog toolkit with pre-built workflows for metrics, logs, monitors, and dashboards.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md) describing workflows for Datadog automation via Rube MCP. All 116 static analysis findings are false positives: backtick detections are Markdown code formatting, not shell execution. The hardcoded URL is documentation for MCP server setup. No executable code present. Network and external command risks are managed through the Rube MCP intermediary service with user-authenticated Datadog connections.

low_findings.title: Documentation References External Service
low_findings.description: Skill documentation references external MCP server endpoint (rube.app) and Datadog API. Users must authenticate separately through official channels.

content.user_title: Automate Datadog Monitoring and Observability Tasks
content.value_statement: Managing Datadog monitoring operations manually is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates metrics queries, log searches, monitor management, and dashboard operations through Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Query Datadog metrics with custom time ranges and tag filters
Search and analyze logs using Datadog query syntax
Create, update, mute, and manage alert monitors
List, view, update, and delete dashboards
Create events and schedule maintenance downtimes
List hosts and retrieve distributed traces
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server connection to be configured separately
Users must authenticate their own Datadog account through official Datadog auth flow
Subject to Datadog API rate limits and data retention policies
Does not support Datadog features outside the Composio toolkit scope

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Incident Response
use_cases.description: Quickly query error logs and metrics during incidents, create monitors for new failure patterns, and mute alerts during planned maintenance windows.

use_cases.title: SRE Dashboard Management
use_cases.description: Create and maintain service health dashboards, set up alert monitors with appropriate thresholds, and manage downtime schedules for deployments.

use_cases.title: Platform Team Observability Setup
use_cases.description: Automate initial monitoring setup for new services including metric queries, log indexes, baseline monitors, and team dashboards.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Metrics Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Query the average CPU usage for host web01 over the last 5 minutes using Datadog metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Log Error Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for all error logs from the payment service in the last hour, sorted by most recent first, limit to 50 entries.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a metric alert monitor named 'High Memory Usage' that triggers when avg memory usage exceeds 85% on production hosts. Send notifications to the ops-slack channel.

prompt_templates.title: Maintenance Downtime
prompt_templates.prompt: Schedule a downtime for all hosts with tag env:staging from 2am to 4am UTC tomorrow with the message 'Scheduled deployment maintenance window'.

output_examples.input: Query CPU usage for web01 last 5 minutes
output_examples.output: Retrieved 60 data points showing average CPU usage ranging from 12% to 45%, with current value at 23%. No anomalies detected in the time series.

output_examples.input: Search error logs for payment service
output_examples.output: Found 127 error logs matching criteria. Top errors: ConnectionTimeout (45%), DatabaseError (30%), ValidationError (25%). Most recent error occurred 2 minutes ago.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing workflows
Use specific tag filters in queries to reduce result noise and improve performance
Set explicit end times for downtimes to avoid indefinite alert suppression
anti_patterns: Do not create monitors without defining clear alert thresholds and notification messages
Avoid querying overly broad time ranges that exceed Datadog retention limits
Do not delete dashboards without confirming backup of widget configurations

faq.question: How do I connect my Datadog account to this skill?
faq.answer: Use the RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS tool with toolkit 'datadog'. If not connected, follow the returned authentication link to complete Datadog OAuth. Confirm status shows ACTIVE before running workflows.

faq.question: What timestamp format should I use for queries?
faq.answer: Most endpoints use Unix epoch seconds (not milliseconds). Some endpoints accept ISO 8601 format. Check the specific tool schema for each endpoint requirements.

faq.question: Can I create custom monitors with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create metric alerts, log alerts, service checks, and query alerts. Ensure the monitor type matches your query type to avoid creation failures.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination for large result sets?
faq.answer: Use the page and page_size parameters or offset-based pagination depending on the endpoint. Check the response for total count to determine if more pages exist.

faq.question: What happens if I hit Datadog rate limits?
faq.answer: Implement exponential backoff on 429 responses. Batch operations where possible and use specific filters to reduce API call volume.

faq.question: Can I mute a monitor without deleting it?
faq.answer: Yes, use the mute monitor function to temporarily silence notifications. The monitor continues evaluating but will not send alerts until unmuted.
